title: crotalaria subcapitata
intro: Crotalaria subcapitata, also known as the Cape rattlebox or rattle bush, is a plant species belonging to the legume family Fabaceae, native to South Africa. Traditionally, Crotalaria subcapitata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against certain types of cancer. In herbal preparations, Crotalaria subcapitata is often used as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also employed in traditional medicine, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
